skill.name: nano-banana-edit
skill.description: Edit images with Google Nano Banana 2 (image-to-image edit endpoint) on RunComfy. Documents Nano Banana Edit's strengths (preserve subject identity, swap background, localize edits with spatial language, multi-image batch edits up to 20 inputs), the schema, and when to route to GPT Image 2 edit / Flux Kontext / Nano Banana 2 t2i instead. Calls `runcomfy run google/nano-banana-2/edit` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "nano banana edit", "edit with nano banana", "image edit nano banana", or any explicit ask to edit with this model.
skill.summary: Edit images with Google Nano Banana 2 (image-to-image) on RunComfy with batch support up to 20 inputs
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation wrapper that calls the RunComfy CLI to invoke the Google Nano Banana 2 image-to-image edit endpoint. Commands shown in documentation (SKILL.md) are markdown code blocks, not actual shell execution. The CLI passes user prompts as JSON via the --input flag, not via shell string interpolation, eliminating command injection risk. The skill properly documents its security architecture including token storage, input boundary handling, and network endpoints.

content.user_title: Edit Images with Google Nano Banana 2 on RunComfy
content.value_statement: AI image editing typically requires complex API calls and batch management. This skill provides Claude with complete knowledge of the Google Nano Banana 2 image-to-image edit endpoint, including prompt engineering best practices, batch processing for up to 20 images, and routing guidance for when to use alternative models.
actual_capabilities: Edit single or batch images (up to 20 inputs) using Google Nano Banana 2 on RunComfy
Preserve subject identity, pose, and clothing while changing backgrounds
Localize edits to specific regions using spatial language (left object, upper-right corner)
Consistent batch editing with locked aspect ratio and resolution
Multi-image variation generation with seed control for reproducibility
Route to GPT Image 2 or Flux Kontext when Nano Banana is not optimal
limitations: 1-20 input images and 1-4 outputs per API call
Long compound prompts may cause drift; split into multiple passes
Web search grounding adds latency and cost
Multilingual in-image text editing performs better with GPT Image 2

use_cases.title: E-commerce product catalog updates
use_cases.description: Swap backgrounds on product images while preserving the product identity, consistent across an entire SKU catalog.

use_cases.title: Marketing creative variations
use_cases.description: Generate A/B variants of ad creative by editing backgrounds or adding localized elements while maintaining brand consistency.

use_cases.title: Influencer content localization
use_cases.description: Replace backgrounds on influencer spokesperson photos for different markets while preserving identity across edits.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and brand teams

prompt_templates.title: Simple background swap
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the subject identity unchanged. Convert the background into a rainy neon cyberpunk street.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user wants to change the background while preserving the main subject

prompt_templates.title: Localized object edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the leftmost object only. Keep the right two objects, the table, and the lighting unchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user wants to edit a specific region without affecting other parts of the image

prompt_templates.title: Text replacement in brand asset
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the bottle, label, and lighting exactly as in the input. Replace only the brand text on the label from "ALPHA" to "AURA", same font weight, centered, white on black.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user wants to update text elements while preserving visual consistency

prompt_templates.title: Batch background consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: For each input image: keep the subject's pose and identity unchanged. Convert the background to a soft warm-grey studio sweep with subtle floor shadow. Center the subject at the same fraction of frame as the input.
prompt_templates.scenario: When the user wants to apply consistent edits across multiple images

output_examples.input: User: Edit this product photo with a minimalist white background
output_examples.output: The skill invokes the RunComfy CLI to call google/nano-banana-2/edit with a prompt structured as: 'Keep the product identity, shape, and labeling unchanged. Convert the background to a clean white minimalist studio backdrop.' The model returns the edited image saved to the specified output directory.

output_examples.input: User: Remove the person on the left from all three photos, keep the right side exactly the same
output_examples.output: The skill calls the edit endpoint with three image URLs and a spatial prompt: 'Remove the leftmost person only. Keep the right side of the image, the background, and all other elements exactly as in the input.' Three edited images are returned with consistent edits applied.

best_practices: Lead prompts with preservation goals before stating changes; models honor what is stated up front
Use concrete spatial language (upper-left, bottom-right) rather than vague terms like 'more modern'
Lock aspect_ratio and resolution when batch editing to ensure consistent framing across outputs
anti_patterns: Compound instructions with multiple unrelated changes cause drift; split into sequential passes
Passive voice edits like 'the background should be changed' reduce effectiveness; use imperative mood
Missing preservation goals allows the model to subtly modify faces or brand elements unintentionally

faq.question: How many images can I edit in one batch?
faq.answer: The Nano Banana 2 edit endpoint accepts 1 to 20 input images per API call. The first image is treated as primary, with the rest providing auxiliary cues for the edit.

faq.question: Can I edit just one part of an image without affecting the rest?
faq.answer: Yes. Use spatial language to localize edits. Examples: 'the left object only', 'upper-right corner', 'background only'. Concrete spatial scopes are honored by the model.

faq.question: How do I get consistent results across a batch?
faq.answer: Lock both aspect_ratio and resolution parameters. Use the same prompt grammar across all images so each output reads as a sibling, not a remix.

faq.question: When should I use this skill instead of GPT Image 2 edit?
faq.answer: Use Nano Banana Edit when you need to preserve subject identity across edits, want batch processing up to 20 images, or need localized edits with spatial language. Use GPT Image 2 when editing multilingual text inside images.

faq.question: What happens if my prompt is too complex?
faq.answer: Long compound instructions increase drift for each scope added. Split complex edits into multiple passes: pass one changes the background only, pass two swaps the subject's outfit.

faq.question: How does the skill handle API authentication?
faq.answer: Authentication uses the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable or runcomfy login which stores tokens in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. The skill does not expose or transmit credentials in prompts.
